title: Health National Adaptation Plans (HNAPs) — African Countries
description: Status of Health National Adaptation Plans (HNAPs) across African countries, showing whether each country has implemented, is considering, or has not yet decided on developing an HNAP.

legend.Implemented: The country has developed and fully implemented a Health National Adaptation Plan (HNAP)
legend.Under Consideration: The country is in the process of developing or integrating an HNAP
legend.Not Yet Decided: The country has not yet initiated the process of developing an HNAP

columns.hnap_status: Current HNAP status: Implemented / Under Consideration / Not Yet Decided
columns.integration_details: Description of how health is being integrated into climate adaptation plans
columns.source_url: Reference URL for additional information (where available)

countries.integration_details: Uganda has developed and implemented a comprehensive HNAP focusing on health system resilience.
South Africa has a National Climate Change and Health Adaptation Plan emphasizing climate-resilient health systems.
Mauritania is in the process of developing its HNAP, focusing on integrating health into national adaptation strategies.
Malawi is facing significant climate-related health challenges and is considering the development of an HNAP to address these issues.
Algeria has implemented an HNAP focusing on climate-resilient health systems.
Angola is developing its HNAP to address climate-related health challenges.
Benin is in the process of integrating health into its national adaptation plans.
Botswana has a comprehensive HNAP emphasizing climate-resilient health infrastructure.
